texto: Sad-looking brown eyes, they wrenched his heart like a gut punch. Worse – hell, worse – a bloke could punch him in the head but he’d stay up, and grin through the bloody split lip, intimidating his attacker; but there was no honour in wounds inside, wounds that only you could deal with.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Karl Drinkwater: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “Sad-looking brown eyes, they wrenched his heart like a gut punch. Worse – hell, worse – a bloke c…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, horror, punch pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.
